title: pilosella × rubra
intro: Pilosella × rubra, also known as the Ruby-leaved Wood Sorrel, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family, resulting from the cross between Pilosella officinarum and Pilosella caespitosa. The medicinal properties of Pilosella × rubra include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, offering health benefits such as antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, as well as the potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. Pilosella × rubra can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can be used in salads or as a garnish, although its use should be limited due to potential side effects and interactions with medications.
